benefits.title: Educators are supported to lead, innovate and influence
benefits.statement: Educators gain a visible, resourced and intellectually generative home, comparable in status and energy to a research centre, through which they can build community and identity, exercise leadership, develop and test ideas, access support and investment, and extend the reach of their work within and beyond King’s.
benefits.beneficiary: Academic and professional service educators, students participating as partners and emerging educational leaders, faculties and services
benefits.successLooksLike: Educators regard the communities as consequential places in which to contribute and lead. Participation creates meaningful relationships, supported opportunities and funded activity, while educational expertise and innovation gain greater institutional visibility, influence and external reach.

benefits.title: King’s can advance cross-cutting priorities through distributed leadership
benefits.statement: King’s gains an authentic institutional route for advancing cross-cutting educational priorities through the collective expertise of the people who teach, support and learn. This enables coherent provisions, practices and pedagogies to emerge with evidence, legitimacy and shared ownership rather than through central prescription.
benefits.beneficiary: Students, educators, faculties, professional services and institutional education leadership
benefits.benefitType: distributed institutional leadership and strategic educational change
benefits.successLooksLike: Strategic educational priorities generate sustained institution-wide participation and purposeful action. Insight from practice informs strategy, governance and investment, while shared approaches are adopted and adapted across King’s because they have been developed collaboratively and are credible in different educational contexts.
